parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ query: '{ issues(filter: { team: { id: { eq: "a545ae90-ba50-4427-83e6-c0ce7dde9528" } }, state: { type: { nin: ["completed", "cancelled"] } } }, first: 250) { nodes { id identifier title url priorityLabel labels { nodes { name } } } } }' }) }}

parameters.jsCode:
const nodes = $input.first().json.data?.issues?.nodes ?? [];
const filtered = nodes.filter(issue =>
  issue.labels?.nodes?.some(l => l.name.toLowerCase().includes('passing'))
);
const count = filtered.length;

const messages = [
  'Somewhere, a developer is refreshing their PR page for the 47th time today. {count} PRs are waiting for your eyes. `/roll` to pick one.',
  'Each community PR reviewed = one open source contributor who doesn\'t quit forever. {count} are waiting. Don\'t let them down.',
  'Fun fact: {count} community PRs were written with love, caffeine, and questionable commit messages. Show them some love — `/roll`.',
  'The universe is vast and cold. But {count} community PRs are warm and ready for review. Be the warmth. `/roll`',
  'Legend says every unreviewed PR adds a year to a contributor\'s waiting time. {count} legends are unfolding right now. Break the curse — `/roll`',
  'Scientists confirm: reviewing a community PR increases reviewer karma by 47%. {count} PRs available. `/roll`',
  '{count} community PRs walk into a bar. The bartender says \'we don\'t serve PRs here.\' Don\'t be that bartender. `/roll`',
  'Plot twist: the community PR you review today might fix the bug that\'s been annoying you for months. {count} available. `/roll`',
  'Each time a community PR gets reviewed, a developer somewhere finally sleeps soundly at night. {count} developers await. `/roll`',
  'Unreviewed PRs don\'t disappear — they just get sadder. {count} of them are feeling existential right now. `/roll` and make a difference.',
  'A community contributor submitted a PR and went to make coffee. They\'re still waiting. {count} PRs, infinite coffees. `/roll`',
  'Every great open source project was built on reviewed PRs and broken dreams. Help with the first part. {count} waiting. `/roll`',
  'Ghost stories for developers: {count} PRs, submitted months ago, never reviewed, drifting through the codebase... `/roll` before it\'s too late.',
  'In a parallel universe, every community PR gets reviewed in 24 hours. Be that universe. {count} waiting. `/roll`',
  'Reviewing a community PR has been scientifically linked to: good vibes, better karma, and at least one contributor not rage-quitting open source. {count} available. `/roll`',
  'If {count} PRs were pizza slices, that\'d be a lot of cold pizza. Don\'t let good contributions go cold. `/roll`',
  'A contributor pushed their first ever PR to Strapi. It\'s in that pile of {count}. Be the reason they come back. `/roll`',
  'The PR queue has {count} items. The coffee machine has 0 excuses. `/roll`',
  'Somewhere a contributor is telling their rubber duck \'don\'t worry, they\'ll review it soon.\' The duck is skeptical. {count} PRs. `/roll`',
  'Community PRs are like plants — ignore them long enough and they die. {count} are looking a little thirsty. `/roll`',
  'Breaking news: {count} community PRs found waiting patiently in Linear. Experts recommend `/roll` as immediate treatment.',
  'Every unreviewed PR is a tiny cry for help wrapped in a diff. {count} tiny cries. `/roll`',
  'A contributor spent their weekend on a PR. You could spend 20 minutes on it. {count} are waiting. `/roll`',
  'Fun activity: `/roll` a PR, review it, feel like a hero. {count} hero opportunities available right now.',
  'The Strapi community has spoken — in code. {count} times. Time to write back. `/roll`',
  '{count} PRs. One `/roll` command. Somewhere, a butterfly effect of merged code begins.',
  'Archaeologists in 3024 will discover {count} unreviewed PRs and wonder what went wrong. Don\'t let them be right. `/roll`',
  'You ever just want to make someone\'s day? There are {count} contributors hoping you will. `/roll`',
  'The queue doesn\'t clear itself. Trust me, we checked. {count} PRs, zero self-reviewing. `/roll`',
  'Plot twist: you ARE the senior dev that contributors are hoping will review their PR. {count} are counting on you. `/roll`',
  'Community PRs are the love language of open source. {count} messages of love, unread. `/roll`',
  'Every merged community PR is a tiny victory for the open web. {count} victories are pending. `/roll`',
  'A wise person once said: \'be the reviewer you wish to see in the world.\' {count} PRs. Be wise. `/roll`',
  'Studies show that teams who review community PRs regularly are 100% cooler than those who don\'t. {count} chances to be cool. `/roll`',
  'Somewhere a contributor refreshed their PR page again. And again. {count} PRs. End the refresh loop. `/roll`',
  'Hot take: the best part of open source is the community. The second best part is reviewing their {count} PRs. `/roll`',
  'If you review a PR in the forest and no one hears it, does the contributor still get a notification? Yes. {count} waiting. `/roll`',
  'Time is a flat circle. The PR queue is not — it has {count} items and it grows. `/roll`',
  'Each community PR reviewed = one less developer who switches to a competitor\'s framework. {count} developers on the edge. `/roll`',
  'The gap between \'submitted\' and \'reviewed\' is where open source dreams go to question themselves. Close the gap. {count} PRs. `/roll`',
  'Not all heroes wear capes. Some just type `/roll` and review a PR. {count} hero moments available.',
  'Fun fact: contributors who get their PR reviewed are 3× more likely to submit another one. {count} future contributors are waiting. `/roll`',
  'The PR queue is judging us silently. {count} items deep. Let\'s do something about it. `/roll`',
  'Dear diary: today I reviewed a community PR and felt genuinely good about it. {count} diary entries waiting to be written. `/roll`',
  'Confession: {count} PRs have been waiting longer than your last Netflix binge. Priorities. `/roll`',
  'What if the real treasure was the community PRs we reviewed along the way? {count} treasures await. `/roll`',
  'In the time it took to read this message, a contributor hit refresh again. {count} PRs. `/roll`',
  'A PR a day keeps the stale queue away. {count} options. `/roll`',
  'Open source runs on caffeine and reviewed PRs. We\'ve got the caffeine covered. {count} PRs need you. `/roll`',
  'The community built something and trusted us with it. {count} times over. Time to show up. `/roll`'
];

const text = messages[Math.floor(Math.random() * messages.length)]
  .replace('{count}', String(count));

return [{ json: { slackResponse: { response_type: 'in_channel', text } } }];
